## 2025-01-22 - [Chart Component XSS]
**Vulnerability:** The `ChartContainer` component in `src/components/ui/chart.tsx` utilized `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` to inject dynamic CSS variables, posing an XSS risk.
**Learning:** Recharts wrapper generated by shadcn/ui contained a legacy pattern for style injection that is insecure. Even though the input is a configuration object rather than direct user text, any potential future inclusion of unsanitized text in those objects could be rendered as HTML and executed.
**Prevention:** Avoid `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` when standard React children can accomplish the same outcome, as standard rendering safely encodes characters, inherently preventing execution of malicious tags.
## 2025-01-22 - [Sensitive Data Logging]
**Vulnerability:** The `AuthGuard` component in `src/components/AuthGuard.tsx` logged the full `userData` object to the console during authentication checks. This object contains sensitive user information like email addresses.
**Learning:** Even in development or debugging flows, sensitive user information should not be logged to the console, as it can be exposed in browser developer tools or automated error/log aggregation services in production if left in the code. This violates the project's strict no-logging policy for sensitive data.
**Prevention:** Avoid logging complete user objects or any objects containing sensitive PII. If debugging is necessary, log specific, non-sensitive fields or simply log the success/failure state of the authentication check.
